---
# AUTOGENERATED. DO NOT EDIT.
title: "OrgPolicyPolicy"
---
This resource configures organization policy constraints for a project,
folder, or organization. Set exactly one of `projectRef`, `folderRef`, or
`organizationRef` to select the parent resource.

<table>
<thead>
<tr>

<th><strong>Value</strong></th>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</table>

      deniedValues:
      - string

  inheritFromParent: boolean

<table class="properties responsive">
<thead>
    <tr>
        <th colspan="2">Fields</th>
    </tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Dry-run policy. Audit-only policy, can be used to monitor how the policy would have impacted the existing and future resources if it's enforced.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.inheritFromParent</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Determines the inheritance behavior for this policy.

 If `inherit_from_parent` is true, policy rules set higher up in the
 hierarchy (up to the closest root) are inherited and present in the
 effective policy. If it is false, then no rules are inherited, and this
 policy becomes the new root for evaluation.
 This field can be set only for policies which configure list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.reset</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Ignores policies set above this resource and restores the `constraint_default` enforcement behavior of the specific constraint at this resource. This field can be set in policies for either list or boolean constraints. If set, `rules` must be empty and `inherit_from_parent` must be set to false.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (object)</code></p>
            <p>In policies for boolean constraints, the following requirements apply:

   - There must be one and only one policy rule where condition is unset.
   - Boolean policy rules with conditions must set `enforced` to the
     opposite of the policy rule without a condition.
   - During policy evaluation, policy rules with conditions that are
     true for a target resource take precedence.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].allowAll</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Setting this to true means that all values are allowed. This field can be set only in policies for list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].condition</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>A condition which determines whether this rule is used in the evaluation of the policy. When set, the `expression` field in the `Expr' must include from 1 to 10 subexpressions, joined by the "||" or "&&" operators. Each subexpression must be of the form "resource.matchTag('<ORG_ID>/tag_key_short_name, 'tag_value_short_name')". or "resource.matchTagId('tagKeys/key_id', 'tagValues/value_id')". where key_name and value_name are the resource names for Label Keys and Values. These names are available from the Tag Manager Service. An example expression is: "resource.matchTag('123456789/environment, 'prod')". or "resource.matchTagId('tagKeys/123', 'tagValues/456')".</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].condition.description</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].condition.expression</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language syntax.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].condition.location</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].condition.title</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the expression.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].denyAll</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Setting this to true means that all values are denied. This field can be set only in policies for list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].enforce</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>If `true`, then the policy is enforced. If `false`, then any configuration is acceptable. This field can be set only in policies for boolean constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].parameters</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Optional. Required for managed constraints if parameters are defined.
 Passes parameter values when policy enforcement is enabled. Each
 field corresponds to a known managed-constraint parameter key; the
 value type matches the type defined in the constraint definition.
 For example, the essentialcontacts.managed.allowedContactDomains
 managed constraint takes:

     parameters:
       allowedDomains:
         - "@example.com"</p>
        </td>
    </tr>

        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].values</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>List of values to be used for this policy rule. This field can be set only in policies for list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].values.allowedValues</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>List of values allowed at this resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].values.allowedValues[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].values.deniedValues</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>List of values denied at this resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>dryRunSpec.rules[].values.deniedValues[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>folderRef</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. The Folder that this resource belongs to. One and only one of 'projectRef', 'folderRef', or 'organizationRef' must be set.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>folderRef.external</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The 'name' field of a folder, when not managed by Config Connector. This field must be set when 'name' field is not set.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>folderRef.name</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The 'name' field of a 'Folder' resource. This field must be set when 'external' field is not set.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>folderRef.namespace</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The 'namespace' field of a 'Folder' resource. If unset, the namespace is defaulted to the namespace of the referencer resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>organizationRef</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. The Organization that this resource belongs to. One and only one of 'projectRef', 'folderRef', or 'organizationRef' must be set.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>organizationRef.external</code></p>
            <p><i>Required*</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The 'name' field of an organization, when not managed by Config Connector.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>projectRef</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Immutable. The Project that this resource belongs to. One and only one of 'projectRef', 'folderRef', or 'organizationRef' must be set.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>projectRef.external</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The `projectID` field of a project, when not managed by Config Connector.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>projectRef.kind</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The kind of the Project resource; optional but must be `Project` if provided.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>projectRef.name</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The `name` field of a `Project` resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>projectRef.namespace</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The `namespace` field of a `Project` resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>resourceID</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>The OrgPolicyPolicy name. If not given, the metadata.name will be used.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Basic information about the Organization Policy.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.inheritFromParent</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Determines the inheritance behavior for this policy.

 If `inherit_from_parent` is true, policy rules set higher up in the
 hierarchy (up to the closest root) are inherited and present in the
 effective policy. If it is false, then no rules are inherited, and this
 policy becomes the new root for evaluation.
 This field can be set only for policies which configure list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.reset</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Ignores policies set above this resource and restores the `constraint_default` enforcement behavior of the specific constraint at this resource. This field can be set in policies for either list or boolean constraints. If set, `rules` must be empty and `inherit_from_parent` must be set to false.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (object)</code></p>
            <p>In policies for boolean constraints, the following requirements apply:

   - There must be one and only one policy rule where condition is unset.
   - Boolean policy rules with conditions must set `enforced` to the
     opposite of the policy rule without a condition.
   - During policy evaluation, policy rules with conditions that are
     true for a target resource take precedence.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].allowAll</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Setting this to true means that all values are allowed. This field can be set only in policies for list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].condition</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>A condition which determines whether this rule is used in the evaluation of the policy. When set, the `expression` field in the `Expr' must include from 1 to 10 subexpressions, joined by the "||" or "&&" operators. Each subexpression must be of the form "resource.matchTag('<ORG_ID>/tag_key_short_name, 'tag_value_short_name')". or "resource.matchTagId('tagKeys/key_id', 'tagValues/value_id')". where key_name and value_name are the resource names for Label Keys and Values. These names are available from the Tag Manager Service. An example expression is: "resource.matchTag('123456789/environment, 'prod')". or "resource.matchTagId('tagKeys/123', 'tagValues/456')".</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].condition.description</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].condition.expression</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language syntax.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].condition.location</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].condition.title</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the expression.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].denyAll</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>Setting this to true means that all values are denied. This field can be set only in policies for list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].enforce</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>If `true`, then the policy is enforced. If `false`, then any configuration is acceptable. This field can be set only in policies for boolean constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Optional. Required for managed constraints if parameters are defined.
 Passes parameter values when policy enforcement is enabled. Each
 field corresponds to a known managed-constraint parameter key; the
 value type matches the type defined in the constraint definition.
 For example, the essentialcontacts.managed.allowedContactDomains
 managed constraint takes:

     parameters:
       allowedDomains:
         - "@example.com"</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowAnyGKEPath</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">boolean</code></p>
            <p>When true, any Google Kubernetes Engine resource path is permitted.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowPaths</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Resource paths that are explicitly allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowPaths[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedAWSAccountIDs</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>AWS account IDs that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedAWSAccountIDs[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedDataSources</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Data sources that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedDataSources[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedDomains</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Contact domains that are allowed by the managed constraint (for example, email domains permitted by essentialcontacts.managed.allowedContactDomains).</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedDomains[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedEgressFQDNs</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Fully qualified domain names that are allowed for egress traffic.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedEgressFQDNs[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedEncryptions</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Encryption types or modes that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedEncryptions[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedMemberSubjects</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Member subjects (for example, users or service accounts) that are allowed.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedMemberSubjects[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedParents</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Parent resources (organizations, folders, or projects) that are allowed.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedParents[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedPreviewFeatures</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Preview features that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedPreviewFeatures[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedPrincipalSets</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Principal sets that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedPrincipalSets[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedProviders</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Providers that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedProviders[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedRetentionDurationSeconds</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>Retention durations (in seconds) that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedRetentionPeriods</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Retention periods that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedRetentionPeriods[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedSchemes</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>URI schemes that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedSchemes[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedServiceList</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Services that are allowed by the managed constraint (service list form).</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedServiceList[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedServices</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Services that are allowed by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.allowedServices[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.defaultAWSProvider</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Default AWS provider used when no explicit provider is specified.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.defaultXMLServiceProvider</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Default XML service provider used when no explicit provider is specified.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.deniedEditions</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Editions that are denied by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.deniedEditions[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.enforcedProjects</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Projects where the managed constraint is enforced.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.enforcedProjects[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.minimumDestroyScheduleDurationInDays</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>Minimum destroy schedule duration, in days, required by the managed constraint.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.nonFIPSMachineTypes</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>Compute Engine machine types that are not FIPS-compliant but are allowed.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].parameters.nonFIPSMachineTypes[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].values</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>List of values to be used for this policy rule. This field can be set only in policies for list constraints.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].values.allowedValues</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>List of values allowed at this resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].values.allowedValues[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].values.deniedValues</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (string)</code></p>
            <p>List of values denied at this resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td>
            <p><code>spec.rules[].values.deniedValues[]</code></p>
            <p><i>Optional</i></p>
        </td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<p>* Field is required when parent field is specified</p>

### Status
#### Schema
```yaml
conditions:
- lastTransitionTime: string
  message: string
  reason: string
  status: string
  type: string
externalRef: string
observedGeneration: integer
observedState:
  dryRunSpec:
    updateTime: string
  spec:
    updateTime: string
```

<table class="properties responsive">
<thead>
    <tr>
        <th colspan="2">Fields</th>
    </tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">list (object)</code></p>
            <p>Conditions represent the latest available observations of the object's current state.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions[]</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p></p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions[].lastTransitionTime</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions[].message</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Human-readable message indicating details about last transition.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions[].reason</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Unique, one-word, CamelCase reason for the condition's last transition.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions[].status</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Status is the status of the condition. Can be True, False, Unknown.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>conditions[].type</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Type is the type of the condition.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>externalRef</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>A unique specifier for the OrgPolicyPolicy resource in GCP.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>observedGeneration</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">integer</code></p>
            <p>ObservedGeneration is the generation of the resource that was most recently observed by the Config Connector controller. If this is equal to metadata.generation, then that means that the current reported status reflects the most recent desired state of the resource.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>observedState</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>ObservedState is the state of the resource as most recently observed in GCP.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>observedState.dryRunSpec</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Dry-run policy. Audit-only policy, can be used to monitor how the policy would have impacted the existing and future resources if it's enforced.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>observedState.dryRunSpec.updateTime</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Output only. The time stamp this was previously updated. This represents the last time a call to `CreatePolicy` or `UpdatePolicy` was made for that policy.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>observedState.spec</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">object</code></p>
            <p>Basic information about the Organization Policy.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
        <td><code>observedState.spec.updateTime</code></td>
        <td>
            <p><code class="apitype">string</code></p>
            <p>Output only. The time stamp this was previously updated. This represents the last time a call to `CreatePolicy` or `UpdatePolicy` was made for that policy.</p>
        </td>
    </tr>
</tbody>
</table>
